The '''Trade Defense Force (TDF)''', also known as '''Trade Defense''',<ref name="Rogue Planet">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref> was the private military arm of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], composed by the Federation's [[Warship/Legends|warships]] and military personnel.

It was created due to the inability of local [[Planetary Security Forces|planetary security forces]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to protect the Trade Federation's interests.

==History==
The Trade Defense Force was created to protect the [[Transport/Legends|transports]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. It was created due to the inability of local [[Planetary Security Forces]] to protect the Trade Federation's interests.<ref name="EGW" />

Around [[214 BBY]], a series of [[Transport/Legends|transport]] businesses in the [[Rseik sector/Legends|Rseik sector]] decided to join the Trade Federation. They were burdened by pirates, and the Defense Force sent a [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] including a [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser|''Captor''-class heavy munitions cruiser]] and two [[Munifex-class light cruiser|''Munifex''-class cruisers]] to stop the pirates, obliterating them.<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-6-showdown-at-naboo|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 6: Showdown at Naboo}}</ref>

Their [[TDF officer|officers]] wore black, gray, and red uniforms with opalescent stripes.<ref name="Rogue Planet" /> The TDF marines were trained on [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]] to defend freighters against boarders and to protect them while in port. The bulk of the [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] assets were [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]], [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]], and [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]]—all of which were fast attack craft intended to drive [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] away from the slower [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighters]]. The fact that it possessed such a fleet disturbed some in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], although the TDF proved that they were effective in a number of early engagements with pirates and raiders, and the Federation also worked closely with the [[Judicial Forces/Legends|Judicial Forces]] against bigger threats. One argument against their effectiveness in direct military action, however, was their assets being constantly spread across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] network of [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]]. However, like its parent organization, it would become more ruthless in exploiting outlying sectors, and later act as the military "will" of the Trade Federation, including forcing concessions with backwater systems with their [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], as well as driving away non-Federation transports from ports, and the [[Kuati/Legends|Kuati]] and Balmorran traditions faded away as soon as the first [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]], [[Gran/Legends|Gran]], and [[Neimoidian]]s began taking over key parts of the Federation. The marines were also largely replaced by [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], with [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class battleships]] also increasingly enforcing their policies.<ref name="EGW" />

During the Federation's occupation of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], the Defense Force temporarily substituted for the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]. After the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] and upon the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|arrest]] of [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], most of the TDF was turned over to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

After the [[Declaration of a New Order]] that reorganized the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and during the Empire's [[Reconquest of the Rim]], the rogue [[Customs Vizier]] [[Marath Vooro]] refused to acknowledge the [[Imperial–Trade Federation treaty|treaty]] that Acting Viceroy [[Sentepeth Findos]] signed regarding handing the Empire control of its resources, with Vooro also gaining control of the remnants of the Trade Defense Force and its droids at [[Enarc/Legends|Enarc]], resulting in the [[Five Veils Campaign]] until Vooro was captured at the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]].<ref name="EGW" />
